chapter_context.summary: Paul corrects a false report that the Day of the Lord had already come. He explains that the 'man of lawlessness' must be revealed first and exhorts them to stand firm in the truth.

outline.text: Events Preceding the Day of the Lord
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Events Preceding the Day of the Lord (2:1-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Thessalonians.

outline.text: A Call to Stand Firm in the Truth
outline.explanation: This section focuses on A Call to Stand Firm in the Truth (2:13-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Thessalonians.

1.commentary:
### Tyndale Study Notes
**Chapter Overview: 2 Thessalonians 2**
This verse contributes to the central message of 2 Thessalonians, emphasizing the grace and truth revealed in Jesus Christ.
1.full_commentary:
### Matthew Henry Commentary
Matthew Henry offers devotional reflections on the spiritual impact of this passage for the believer.

### Jamieson-Fausset-Brown
JFB analysis explores the historical context and original language nuances of this portion of the New Testament.

key_themes.title: The Day of the Lord
key_themes.description: Clarifying the timing and events of Christ's return.

devotional.question: How does this passage impact your daily walk with God?

cultural_historical.title: New Testament Context
cultural_historical.text: Written in the 1st century AD to diverse groups of believers across the Roman Empire.
